title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, say why the people might be sleeping in these different places, and what might wake them up.

images.label: A
images.description: An exhausted passenger sleeping upright on a train or bus, leaning their head against the window or seat.

images.label: B
images.description: A camper sleeping inside a small tent in a forest, visible through the unzipped entrance of the tent.

images.label: C
images.description: A young child or toddler sleeping deeply and comfortably in a clean, soft bed with blankets at home.

followUp.question: Who do you think will be able to spend the longest time asleep? (Why?)

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, say why the people might be taking the photos in these situations, and how patient the photographers need to be.

images.label: D
images.description: A wildlife photographer camouflaged in nature, using a very large professional telephoto lens to photograph a bird.

images.label: E
images.description: A tourist standing in a crowded public square taking a photo of a famous historical monument with a smartphone.

images.label: F
images.description: A person at a social gathering or wedding taking a group photo of friends laughing and posing together.

followUp.question: In which situation do you think it is most difficult to get the photo right? (Why?)
